seo_description: Current time in Lagos, Nigeria — live West Africa Time (WAT, UTC+1) clock, no DST, and time differences with London, New York, and world cities.

faq.question: What time is it in Lagos right now?
faq.answer: Lagos, Nigeria uses West Africa Time (WAT, UTC+1) year-round. Nigeria does not observe Daylight Saving Time. Lagos is Africa's largest city by population and the economic capital of Nigeria. The IANA timezone identifier is Africa/Lagos.

faq.question: What time zone is Lagos in?
faq.answer: Lagos is in the West Africa Time zone (WAT, UTC+1). Nigeria uses UTC+1 year-round with no DST. Lagos shares its timezone with many West and Central African cities including Kinshasa, Luanda, Libreville, and Bangui. The IANA identifier is Africa/Lagos.

faq.question: Does Lagos observe daylight saving time?
faq.answer: No. Nigeria does not observe Daylight Saving Time. Lagos remains on WAT (UTC+1) year-round. This is the same offset as Central European Time (CET) in winter, making Lagos well-positioned for business with European firms — the clocks are identical to Frankfurt and Paris during November–March.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Lagos and London?
faq.answer: Lagos (WAT, UTC+1) is 1 hour ahead of London (GMT, UTC+0) in UK winter. During UK summer (BST, UTC+1), Lagos and London are on the same time. Since Lagos has no DST, the gap changes only when London transitions: 1 hour in UK winter, 0 hours in UK summer.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Lagos and New York?
faq.answer: Lagos (WAT, UTC+1) is 6 hours ahead of New York (EST, UTC-5) in winter. During US Daylight Saving Time (EDT, UTC-4), Lagos is 5 hours ahead. Since Nigeria has no DST, the gap changes only when the US transitions clocks.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Lagos and Johannesburg?
faq.answer: Lagos (WAT, UTC+1) is 1 hour behind Johannesburg (SAST, UTC+2). Neither Nigeria nor South Africa observes DST, so this 1-hour gap is constant year-round. When it is 9:00 AM in Lagos, it is 10:00 AM in Johannesburg.

faq.question: What are typical business hours in Lagos?
faq.answer: Standard business hours in Lagos are 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM WAT, Monday through Friday. Lagos is Nigeria's commercial capital and Africa's largest city by population (~20 million people). The Nigerian Stock Exchange (NGX) operates 10:00 AM–2:30 PM WAT. Lagos is a major hub for banking, tech (Silicon Lagoon), oil/gas (with proximity to Niger Delta), and media.
